widgetData.source_sentence: Represent the plot: A celebrated military contractor (Bradley Cooper) returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs and reconnects with a long-time love (PERSON), while unexpectedly a female ORG fighter pilot captain (PERSON) is assigned to him.
sentences: Represent the plot: Military contractor PERSON returns to GPE accompanying billionaire entrepreneur PERSON, who intends to develop local land into a space center. Following a celebrated military career that ended in shadowy deals in GPE, the now-disillusioned ORG has to negotiate a deal with the Native Hawaiians and support PERSON's launch of a privately funded satellite. His mission is complicated by his former girlfriend PERSON, now married with two children, and his idealistic ORG liaison, Captain PERSON, whose wide-eyed fascination with space reminds him of his own childhood. Gilcrest and ORG meet King Dennis PERSON at his isolated community, asking for him to participate in a blessing ceremony that will allow PERSON to build his center. Having unsuccessfully attempted to probe ORG's background, ORG, who is of partial Hawaiian background, connects with the king and his companions, as she shares their spiritual view of the land and sky. Eventually, with her assistance, Gilcrest brokers a settlement with the king allowing for control over the territory of two whole mountains and free cell phone service for the area. The following night, PERSON and Ng have dinner at PERSON's house, where they meet her husband Woody and children, PERSON and PERSON. Later, alone in the kitchen, PERSON confesses to Gilcrest she planned a life with him before he abandoned her thirteen years earlier. The next evening, at ORG, the commander of ORG, General PERSON, warns Gilcrest against ruining their deal. One of his men hands Gilcrest a thumb drive containing top secret information for the upcoming satellite launch. During the party, Gilcrest becomes attracted to ORG, who is having fun dancing with PERSON. Afterwards, Ng joins Gilcrest in his hotel room while he recounts his near-death experiences in GPE. The next day, Ng discovers that the satellite will actually carry a nuclear payload. When she tries to resign, she is told that it is a private operation run by PERSON and that PERSON is aware of the details. She later tearfully confronts PERSON for deceiving her and the Hawaiians. Meanwhile, PERSON and PERSON confront each other about recent marital tensions, which he believes started as soon as ORG arrived, and both agree to separate. The next morning, she arrives at the hotel and reveals that PERSON is his daughter. Later that day, after the successful blessing of ORG, PERSON learns that the Chinese are attempting to hack the satellite's code to prevent the impending launch. He rushes to the command center and undermines the hackers' efforts. As he watches the satellite enter geosynchronous orbit, he realizes his actions have impacted Ng, whom he has come to love. Quickly, he orders a massive sonic upload to be sent to space, and both watch the satellite explode. Believing her continued association with him will ruin her promising career, he insists that they should end their romantic relationship. PERSON, displeased that his satellite has been destroyed, confronts PERSON, who reminds him that the sky is meant to be shared by all, and PERSON, also incensed by ORG's actions, threatens to prosecute him. When PERSON returns to PERSON's, she reads him a moving love letter from PERSON. He tells her she belongs with her husband, and she encourages him to pursue Ng. Arriving home, Woody notices Gilcrest, tells him he knows he is ORG's father, and asks him if he slept with PERSON while he was away, to which he responds that he slept with ORG. As PERSON is cleaning up the living room, she notices Woody and they happily embrace each other, with PERSON and PERSON joining them. PERSON soon realizes PERSON was being truthful about the satellite carrying a nuclear weapons payload, praises his actions, and reveals that authorities have arrested PERSON and will soon be taking him into custody. Outside the hotel, Gilcrest finds Ng, who is preparing to leave, professes his love to her, and promises to await her return to GPE. Later that night, Gilcrest watches ORG dance during her hula class. She notices him nodding at her, suddenly realizing that he is her father. With tears of joy in her eyes, she runs outside and embraces him before returning to her class.

Represent the plot: 13-year-old orphan PERSON is living in servitude with the cruel PERSON family in GPE. After Mr. PERSON dies, she is sent back to the orphanage and later learns that she has been adopted by a family in GPE. Upon arriving in the small town of GPE, she is met at the train station by the elderly PERSON, who is surprised to find her there. PERSON and his sister, PERSON had requested a boy to help them on their farm, GPE, but PERSON was sent by mistake. Marilla puts PERSON on trial before deciding whether to keep her or send her back. Shortly thereafter, PERSON loses her temper with PERSON's friend and town gossip PERSON, who criticizes her looks and red hair. She refuses to apologize, preferring to go back to the orphanage, but PERSON convinces her to do so. PERSON accepts the apology and suggests to PERSON that PERSON attend a Sunday School picnic so she can meet other children. At the picnic, PERSON becomes bosom friends with PERSON from across the pond. She also meets PERSON, who shows an interest in her. At school, PERSON tries to get her attention by making fun of her red hair and calling her Carrots, and she angrily smashes a slate over his head. She vows never to forgive him and tries to salvage her wounded pride by dying her hair black, but it instead turns green. After finding out what happened, Marilla decides that PERSON will stay at LOC. PERSON invites PERSON to a tea party, where she accidentally serves currant wine instead of raspberry cordial and gets PERSON drunk. Mrs. PERSON thinks it was intentional and forbids the girls from seeing each other again. Meanwhile, Miss PERSON becomes the new GPE schoolteacher. She wants PERSON to join a special class for students who plan to take the entrance exam for ORG. While PERSON is away, PERSON arrives in a panic and tells PERSON and PERSON that her little sister PERSON is sick with croup. PERSON treats the girl with ipecac and saves her life. Mrs. PERSON is grateful and invites PERSON to a Christmas ball in PERSON overnight. PERSON sees PERSON at the dance but is not willing to befriend him. She also meets PERSON, who invites her and PERSON to visit her mansion in GPE, where PERSON takes the PERSON's entrance exam. Back in GPE, PERSON and her friends recreate PERSON The Lady of PERSON, with PERSON playing the role of Elaine the Lily Maid. While floating down the river, her boat springs a leak and sinks, leaving her helplessly clinging to a bridge. PERSON comes rowing by and rescues her, then tells her they have tied for first place on the exam. He also asks her to forgive him for what he said before and insists he wants to be her friend, but PERSON is still hesitant. PERSON heads to ORG to continue her education at PERSON's and obtain a teacher's license. She finds out that the graduate who receives the highest mark in English literature wins the ORG scholarship, $250 for four years, which she plans to use to get an arts degree at ORG. When the results come in, she is the winner of the scholarship. Returning to GPE, she finds out that PERSON's father cannot afford to send him to college, so he will be teaching at the GPE school to earn his way. After PERSON passes away, Marilla considers selling ORG since her eyesight is failing and she may go blind. However, PERSON reveals that she has declined the scholarship. Instead of going to GPE, she will study by correspondence while teaching school in Carmody. Marilla reveals that PERSON's father was an old beau with whom she quarreled, and she wishes she had forgiven him when she had the chance. Later, PERSON explains that he asked the trustees to let PERSON have the GPE school and give ORG to him, so PERSON can stay at LOC and help Marilla. He will also be taking courses by correspondence and asks for PERSON's help in studying. The two friends make up for good and PERSON walks PERSON home.

Represent the plot: Saga returns to his village after a long absence, and finds that his father has married ORG, his fiancee, during his leave.  PERSON has become his second wife, and by law, ORG's mother. Saga runs away and builds a straw hut near the village. Still in love, Saga and ORG begin an affair, with ORG telling her parents she is going to visit her aunt, then running to Saga's hut. After the affair is discovered, ORG's father decrees that he must die for dishonoring the family. ORG's father hangs himself from a tree, and ORG is disowned by her mother at her father's funeral. Saga's brother PERSON is selected to execute Saga. He pretends to kill Saga so as to restore the family's honor. Saga and PERSON then run away to another village, and the family falls apart. As Saga and PERSON begin to build a life, ORG tells Saga that she is pregnant. Meanwhile, PERSON comes to regret his failure to kill Saga. After ORG's birth mother dies, Saga returns to the village, exposing PERSON's failure to carry out his father's orders. PERSON's father tells him he is banished. Kougri then picks up ORG's rifle and shoots him for having brought ruin to the family and his own life. He then walks off into exile and probable death.

widgetData.source_sentence: Represent the plot: Osuofia (Nkem Owoh), a colorful Nigerian villager, receives news of his brother ORG's death in GPE, GPE. However, in his will, ORG has left his vast fortune to Osuofia as the sole beneficiary. Osuofia then travels to GPE and discovers that his brother's English fiancée, PERSON (Mara Derwent), is hesitant to follow the Nigerian tradition of inheriting the estate. These cultural misunderstandings lead to a series of amusing situations that further strengthen the family bonds between ORG, his wife, and his daughter.
sentences: Represent the plot: synopsis PERSON (PERSON) and PERSON (PERSON) are a young couple, married for only six months, but already facing issues that prevent them from enjoying a fulfilling sex life. In their attempt to address and resolve the issue, they reach out to marriage counsellor Alfred (Spalding Gray). During the process, PERSON is shocked to find out that GPE has never had a genuine orgasm when they had sex with each other. Meanwhile, PERSON, who is not happy with the results of PERSON's traditional psychoanalytic approach, reaches out to Baltazar (Terence Stamp). PERSON works as an architect at a construction site. One day, while at work, PERSON's site colleagues invite him to spy on an old man who is having sex with The ORG. It is only when he decides to look through the telescope lens that he finds out that one of the women is PERSON. The old man is PERSON, an alternative sex therapist who engages in sex with his patients as part of his technique. PERSON, furious, confronts Balthazar about him and his wife, but in a turn of events he becomes a patient himself. During one of his attempts to practice the techniques taught by PERSON, PERSON causes PERSON to suffer a seizure, which results in her hospitalization. At that time it is revealed that her sex life dysfunction is linked to the sexual abuse that she suffered as a child, perpetrated by her father. She is now called to confront the memories of her past in order to recover.

Represent the plot: On Friday of homecoming weekend at ORG, a leading historically black college in GPE, GPE, ORG “Dap” Dunlap, a politically militant and socially conscious black senior leads an anti-apartheid demonstration, demanding that the school divest from GPE. However, the fraternity PERSON and their pledge class, the ORG, arrive to interrupt the demonstration. PERSON” Eaves, the head/president of ORG PERSON chapter and ORG's former friend, upsets ORG by stating his opposition to the protest, after which ORG president PERSON breaks up both their subsequent fight and the rally. ORG's younger cousin ORG, a PERSON pledge known as “Half-Pint” to Julian and his fellow ORG (GPE, Dr. PERSON, Lance, X-Ray Vision, and General PERSON), is ordered to bring a girl to the fraternity that night, and goes to Dap for advice. PERSON, chairman of ORG's board of trustees, warns college president PERSON that ORG's divestment protests may scare off the school's wealthy donors. In the evening, ORG asks his friends PERSON (GPE, GPE, GPE, Edge, and PERSON) to return with him to the administration building to protest. Although supportive of ORG’s cause, they decline, waning to complete their college education and get good jobs in the future. Meanwhile, the Gamma Rays (wannabes, who are mostly light-skinned black women with straightened hair), the PERSON women's auxiliary led by PERSON's girlfriend PERSON, clash with some of their non-Greek classmates (jigaboos, who are mostly dark-skinned black women with natural hair), including ORG's girlfriend PERSON, over skin color and hair politics. Unsuccessful in courting any female students, ORG and his fellow PERSON (Doo-Doo Breath, Double Rubber, PERSON, PERSON, Yoda, PERSON, and Sir PERSON) are hazed by ORG. Dap and PERSON have a falling out when she plans to rush a sorority, and she accuses him of colorism. On Saturday, the weekend's festivities begin, and the PERSON brothers nearly come to blows with ORG and his fellow protestors at the homecoming parade. After the Mission football team suffers an embarrassing loss at the homecoming football game, ORG is called into ORG’s office and informed by PERSON that if he continues with his protests he will be expelled. Da PERSON drive into town to eat at a local ORG, where they are harassed by locals who resent them as privileged college boys. Returning to campus, ORG confronts Julian about ORG's pledge status. On Sunday, at the Greeks' step show, a performance by PERSON (as the brothers of ORG) leads to a brawl with the PERSON. Seeking out Rachel, Dap is humiliated by her dorm neighbors, but he and PERSON reconcile. That evening, following a grueling initiation, the PERSON are welcomed as new members. At the school dance, ORG's roommate GPE hits it off with a female student and coaxes her to his room, but she refuses to stay when ORG and PERSON are already there. As the PERSON celebrate, Julian becomes annoyed with PERSON. Singling out Half-Pint, Julian orders him to sleep with PERSON as a final test. Although ORG is uncomfortable with PERSON's request, PERSON insists she would do anything for Julian. Afterward, Julian accuses PERSON of prioritizing the fraternity and his position as leader over him, and breaks up with her. ORG excitedly informs Dap that he slept with PERSON by PERSON’s orders; infuriated that his cousin has become an entitled PERSON, Dap disowns him. At sunrise, ORG runs to the center of the campus quad and awakens the entire campus from the previous night's debauchery by ringing a large bell. A tearful Julian, remorseful of his treatment towards Half-Pint and PERSON, arrives and stands eye-to-eye with ORG, who breaks the fourth wall to tell the audience directly, “Please, wake up.”

widgetData.source_sentence: Represent the plot: The womanizing Count Almaviva meets PERSON in GPE, and they are instantly attracted to each other. PERSON's guardian, PERSON, who hopes to marry her and continue controlling her fortune, takes her to GPE to separate the lovers. The Count follows them there, and he is followed by PERSON, his former lover, whose husband has begun proceedings to annul their marriage. Almaviva enlists the help of ORG, a resourceful Sevillian barber, who is in turn aided by a beautiful gypsy. Meanwhile, PERSON bribes PERSON, a corrupt clergyman, to expedite his marriage to PERSON. After several incidents, PERSON and PERSON marry, and Figaro convinces PERSON not to contest the marriage. The guardian eventually marries PERSON, and Figaro marries the gypsy.
sentences: Represent the plot: As described in a film magazine, PERSON (PERSON) of GPE, GPE, reads of the German war atrocities and decides to enlist in the British army, thus becoming a forerunner of the American forces that are subsequently to leave for the battlefields of LOC. He begins active training at a camp outside GPE. While enjoying a few hours of leave, he meets PERSON (Gish), a young woman from GPE. She is flattered by his attentions and their friendship soon blossoms into love. PERSON's one dissipation consists of walking in PERSON with her soldier boy. She falls heir to 20,000 pounds and at once becomes the object of much solicitude from Sir PERSON (Walthall), a fortune hunter. When PERSON is ordered with his regiment to go to the ORG, he has no time to bid her adieu. Sir PERSON seeks to force his marriage before he leaves for GPE on a business trip, and she accepts him. German plotters plan to destroy an arsenal at night and Sir PERSON is inveigled into driving an automobile along a GPE road with its lights turned skyward to guide the PERSON. PERSON, wounded and home on furlough, detects Sir PERSON on the lonely road, follows and traps him in his cottage. Sir PERSON turns his pistol on himself rather than be taken alive. PERSON finds the great love in service for the cause of democracy and her country, with a greater love in sight.

Represent the plot: Endre, a ORG at an abattoir, and GPE, the newly hired quality inspector, experience a recurring dream of being a pair of deer in the forest, though they are not aware that it is a shared dream. Mária is immediately unpopular at work for her autistic behavior and uncompromising grading of the abattoir's meat quality. Though Endre tries to befriend her, she quickly becomes uncomfortable with the interaction and rudely comments on his lame left arm, believing it may be a factor into his favorite food in the cafeteria. However, she repeats the conversation to herself that night, analyzing where she made her mistakes. Meanwhile, the abattoir hires a new butcher, GPE, who PERSON takes a quick dislike to due to his cocky demeanor and unsympathetic view towards the slaughtered animals. The abattoir is put under investigation when mating powder is stolen from the inventory; PERSON and his friend PERSON both suspect that GPE is the culprit. A psychologist is hired to perform personality tests on the workers to discover the culprit. The workers are asked questions on the history of their sexuality and physical development, as well as what they dreamed the night before. When PERSON and Mária both report the same dream, the psychologist assumes they are playing a prank. Though Endre and Mária are both skeptical, they realize that they are indeed experiencing the same dream and grow closer. Though PERSON's behavior temporarily drives PERSON away, they eventually form a tight bond. PERSON also learns that PERSON stole the mating powder, but chooses not to inform the police as there are no victims, and he apologizes to GPE for suspecting him. Endre and Mária decide to fall asleep in the same room one night, but both are unable to sleep. Although she loves him, Mária shuts down when PERSON touches her after a night of playing cards, leaving PERSON offended and confused. The incident affects GPE, and she begins to open herself up to new experiences and sensations, such as listening to romantic music, watching pornography, and observing couples at the park. However, PERSON has grown pessimistic about their budding relationship and calls it off. He sleeps with another woman, though the encounter leaves him disappointed. A devastated Mária prepares to commit suicide at home, calmly slitting her wrist in the bathtub. The suicide is interrupted by PERSON calling her, and after a short and awkward conversation, he reveals that he loves her, which Mária reciprocates. After getting her wound bandaged, she goes to PERSON's home, where they make love. After falling asleep, they wake up to realize that neither of them dreamed the night before.

Represent the plot: PERSON, a heroin addict, is on a quest to score more drugs. PERSON has a problematic relationship with his on-off, sexually frustrated girlfriend, PERSON. During the course of the day, PERSON overdoses in front of an upper-class couple, attempts to fool welfare into approving his methadone treatment by having PERSON fake a pregnancy, and frustrates the women in his life with his drug-induced impotence.

widgetData.source_sentence: Represent the plot: A gambler recovering from the addiction that has led him to the streets of GPE, PERSON (PERSON) takes on odd jobs that provide security to support his addiction.
sentences: Represent the plot: The story is divided into four quarters, each one representing a different period in the lives of the protagonists. The first quarter takes place in 1981, when PERSON and GPE are children and become friends due to their shared love for basketball. PERSON proves to be an excellent player, beating PERSON in their first game of one on one. However, their friendship is threatened when PERSON knocks PERSON down during a game, accidentally cutting her face. They reconcile and share their first kiss, but their relationship is complicated. The second quarter is set in 1988 when PERSON and PERSON are attending ORG. PERSON is a star basketball player, and PERSON is the leader of the girls' basketball team. Quincy is popular and dates the prettiest girl in school, while PERSON struggles to control her emotions on the court and harbors feelings for GPE. However, they manage to connect romantically and make love after both being accepted at ORG (ORG). The third quarter begins when they start college, and PERSON is a promising player on the court while PERSON frequently has run-ins with her head coach PERSON and struggles to get playing time on the women's team. Quincy struggles to deal with the media attention and discovers his father's infidelity. The pressures of their athletic and academic commitments, coupled with their deteriorating relationship, cause them to break up. The fourth quarter follows the plot to 1993, a few years before the establishment of the WNBA. PERSON and PERSON are both professional basketball players. PERSON plays for an ORG team in GPE while PERSON is in his fifth year as a player for ORG. After visiting PERSON in the hospital following a devastating knee injury, PERSON learns he is engaged to be married. She also confronts her mother, PERSON, about their troubled past. Quincy recovers from his injury and his wedding draws closer, while PERSON quits basketball to work at a bank. PERSON encourages PERSON to pursue her dreams and PERSON and PERSON play a high stakes one-on-one game, with PERSON agreeing to call off the wedding if he loses. Although PERSON wins, he realizes he can't live without PERSON and chooses her instead. The two get married and have a daughter, with PERSON eventually playing in the WNBA. In a post-credits scene, PERSON and PERSON's daughter is shown playing basketball at a playground.

Represent the plot:  On a snowy day in GPE, PERSON, a young nurse, is being interviewed to care for the wife of PERSON, the owner of a sugar plantation on the LOC island of GPE. Although she is not told much about the patient, PERSON looks forward to living in a warmer climate and accepts the job, laughing it off when asked if she believes in witchcraft. The coachman who takes PERSON to PERSON's house, Fort Holland, tells her the ORG population of PERSON is descended from slaves brought there by PERSON's ancestors. At her first dinner, PERSON meets PERSON's younger half-brother and employee, PERSON, who, though good-humored, clearly resents PERSON. She encounters PERSON, her patient, wandering the grounds of Fort Holland that night, and is initially frightened by the nonverbal, affectless woman, who, as Dr. PERSON later informs PERSON, was left without the willpower to speak or act by herself after a tropical fever irreparably damaged her spinal cord. PERSON learns from a calypso musician's song that PERSON kept PERSON from running away with PERSON right before she became sick. The issues surrounding PERSON have driven PERSON to drink, and the simmering tension between him and PERSON frequently threatens to boil over. PERSON apologizes to PERSON for bringing her to PERSON and admits he feels responsible for PERSON's condition. PERSON, who has fallen in love with PERSON, becomes determined to make him happy by curing PERSON and gets him to agree to a risky insulin shock treatment. When that fails, PERSON, PERSON's maid, convinces PERSON that she should take PERSON to be healed by the houngan (Vodou priest) at the houmfort (Vodou temple). After a man dressed in black performs a ritual dance using a small sword, PERSON gets in line at the houmfort to ask the spirit PERSON to heal PERSON. Instead, she is pulled inside a hut and is shocked to see Mrs. PERSON, PERSON and PERSON's mother, who works with ORG. Mrs. PERSON reveals that, with the houngans knowledge, she has been telling the islanders that Vodou spirits speak through her so they will comply with her medical and sanitary recommendations. Meanwhile, PERSON attracts the attention of the man in black, who stabs her in the arm. She does not bleed, causing murmurs of zombie among the onlookers, and Mrs. PERSON tells PERSON to take PERSON back to Fort Holland. An upset ORG greets them, but he softens when he learns PERSON was trying to help him and lets her know he no longer loves his wife. The ORG congregation demands PERSON be delivered to them for further ritualistic tests, so ORG and the local authorities want her sent to an asylum on a different island. PERSON resists because PERSON wants PERSON to stay, and he tells PERSON to return to GPE before he makes her as unhappy as he made PERSON before her illness. At night, ORG, a zombie who guards a crossroads, is sent to retrieve PERSON, approaching PERSON instead after she puts on PERSON's robe, but Mrs. PERSON orders him to leave. Dr. PERSON visits Fort Holland to report there will be an official investigation to determine PERSON's fate. Faced with scandal, and with her sons at each other's throats, Mrs. PERSON says PERSON is not sick or insane, but dead, as she went to the houmfort the night PERSON tried to run away with PERSON and, possessed, asked the houngan to make PERSON a zombie. Only PERSON believes the story, and he later asks PERSON to euthanize PERSON, though PERSON refuses. Using a small effigy of PERSON, the man in black and the houngan attempt to draw her to the houmfort. PERSON and PERSON stop her the first time, but PERSON helps her the second, following with an arrow removed from a garden statue depicting PERSON that was once the figurehead of a GPE-family slave ship. When the man in black stabs the doll with a pin, PERSON stabs PERSON with the arrow and, pursued slowly by ORG, carries her body into the sea. PERSON and PERSON are discovered floating in the surf, and their bodies are brought back to GPE. PERSON and PERSON console each other.

Represent the plot: PERSON is a recovering gambling addict who takes odd jobs in GPE as a chaperone (his version of a bodyguard) to support his addiction. After helping a client impress a girl, ORG accepts a proposition from a young man named PERSON  to show him around LOC and provide protection while he gambles. While eating at a diner, ORG's waitress friend PERSON hands him a message from his friend PERSON, who wants him to stop by her house. Holly is a professional escort, who explains that she had a date the previous night at the Golden Nugget. After the date, she was brutally assaulted and beaten by three unknown folks in their hotel room. Holly requests PERSON to find their identities in order to sue them.  ORG discovers that the one who is responsible for assaulting PERSON is a gangster named PERSON, who had two of his thugs dump her in a hospital car park. PERSON goes to the hotel to confront PERSON and a confrontation develops, resulting in GPE overpowering PERSON and his men, who are tied up. PERSON calls GPE, who contemplates castrating PERSON, who then breaks down and begs her forgiveness. Holly decides to just take the $50,000 from ORG's desk and leaves. Holly splits the money with ORG and leaves GPE. PERSON takes Cyrus to a casino and whilst playing blackjack with dealer friend PERSON, GPE goes on a huge winning streak with the next dealer, amassing over a half a million dollars, but has a sudden anxiety attack while going to the cashier and decides to keep on gambling, eventually losing all his winnings (as well as his original $25,000) on a single blackjack bet with PERSON. The next morning, PERSON, who is revealed to be a self-made millionaire, wants ORG to mentor him on being brave, but ORG declines. At the bar, PERSON's boys arrive to deliver GPE to PERSON, but ORG fends them off. PERSON meets with PERSON, who is the mafia boss of GPE. Baby has received a complaint from ORG, who claims that ORG broke into his hotel room, pistol-whipped him, and killed two of his men – all to fund his gambling addiction. Baby takes ORG to a room with PERSON, where ORG tells his side of the story: that PERSON killed his own guys, to save face after begging for his life, and that ORG bears a cut on his sex. Baby tells PERSON to drop his pants to prove PERSON wrong, but he refuses and leaves.  At the local diner, Cyrus offers PERSON a check for $500,000 and a plane ticket to Corsica for what he has learned from GPE. PERSON and his crew appear in the diner. Cyrus shows his newfound manliness by singing loudly as a distraction so ORG can escape. PERSON thinks about his sailboat and then kills the thugs and PERSON with his utensils behind the diner. Afterwards, Cyrus insists PERSON take the check and the ticket, where ORG accepts and drives out of GPE.

widgetData.source_sentence: Represent the plot: After leading the ORG team to the local ice hockey championship, coach PERSON returns to help them achieve something as seemingly impossible as winning ORG in GPE, an international championship where the young ORG will have the responsibility of representing GPE.
sentences: Represent the plot: Former PERSON hockey coach PERSON is a star in the minor leagues, expected to reach ORG. However, a career-ending knee injury brings him back to the ORG district of GPE. GPE is offered a chance to coach a team representing GPE in the Junior Goodwill Games in GPE. He manages to reunite most of his former ORG players, while the ORG try to enact revenge for their humiliating loss two years earlier. Their plans are foiled by GPE, who leaves them tied up in their underpants. ORG consists of many of the old ORG, in addition to five new players with special talents. In GPE, the lure of celebrity distracts GPE, who begins to neglect the team for a luxurious lifestyle. The team wins easy victories over GPE and GPE and GPE in the double-elimination tournament. ORG and PERSON gain recognition for their enforcer skills, and are dubbed ORG. Backup goaltender PERSON asks GPE for a chance to play, but is told to wait as PERSON is on a hot streak. The team suffers an embarrassing 12–1 defeat against GPE, coached by ex-NHL player PERSON. USA plays badly, and star center PERSON is slashed in the wrist. Frustrated, GPE drives his players even harder, but they begin to suffer from complete exhaustion. Realizing the children are too tired to complete their school work or even stay awake in class, the team's tutor PERSON intervenes, cancelling practice and confronting GPE over his thoughtlessness. Once better rested, the players encounter a street hockey team who teaches them to play like the real ORG. GPE continues to suffer from the pressure until Jan, brother of ORG's mentor PERSON, visits and reminds him of his love for the game. In their match against GPE, GPE fails to arrive on time, forcing PERSON to tell the referee PERSON is the team's assistant coach. The team struggles, entering the third period tied, until GPE arrives and apologizes to the team for his behavior. Inspired by the true return of their coach, the players win the game with their signature ORG, and advance to the next round. The renewed GPE finally realizes PERSON's wrist injury and benches him despite his complaints. To fill the open roster spot, PERSON recruits street hockey player PERSON, whose unique knucklepuck – which rotates end over end rather than spinning around its centerline – secures GPE's victory over GPE, advancing them to the championship game for a rematch against GPE. PERSON's injury is healed only to find ORG with a full roster. widget.source_sentence: Represent the plot: A celebrated military contractor (Bradley Cooper) returns to the site of his greatest career triumphs and reconnects with a long-time love (PERSON), while unexpectedly a female ORG fighter pilot captain (PERSON) is assigned to him.
sentences: Represent the plot: Military contractor PERSON returns to GPE accompanying billionaire entrepreneur PERSON, who intends to develop local land into a space center. Following a celebrated military career that ended in shadowy deals in GPE, the now-disillusioned ORG has to negotiate a deal with the Native Hawaiians and support PERSON's launch of a privately funded satellite. His mission is complicated by his former girlfriend PERSON, now married with two children, and his idealistic ORG liaison, Captain PERSON, whose wide-eyed fascination with space reminds him of his own childhood. Gilcrest and ORG meet King Dennis PERSON at his isolated community, asking for him to participate in a blessing ceremony that will allow PERSON to build his center. Having unsuccessfully attempted to probe ORG's background, ORG, who is of partial Hawaiian background, connects with the king and his companions, as she shares their spiritual view of the land and sky. Eventually, with her assistance, Gilcrest brokers a settlement with the king allowing for control over the territory of two whole mountains and free cell phone service for the area. The following night, PERSON and Ng have dinner at PERSON's house, where they meet her husband Woody and children, PERSON and PERSON. Later, alone in the kitchen, PERSON confesses to Gilcrest she planned a life with him before he abandoned her thirteen years earlier. The next evening, at ORG, the commander of ORG, General PERSON, warns Gilcrest against ruining their deal. One of his men hands Gilcrest a thumb drive containing top secret information for the upcoming satellite launch. During the party, Gilcrest becomes attracted to ORG, who is having fun dancing with PERSON. Afterwards, Ng joins Gilcrest in his hotel room while he recounts his near-death experiences in GPE. The next day, Ng discovers that the satellite will actually carry a nuclear payload. When she tries to resign, she is told that it is a private operation run by PERSON and that PERSON is aware of the details. She later tearfully confronts PERSON for deceiving her and the Hawaiians. Meanwhile, PERSON and PERSON confront each other about recent marital tensions, which he believes started as soon as ORG arrived, and both agree to separate. The next morning, she arrives at the hotel and reveals that PERSON is his daughter. Later that day, after the successful blessing of ORG, PERSON learns that the Chinese are attempting to hack the satellite's code to prevent the impending launch. He rushes to the command center and undermines the hackers' efforts. As he watches the satellite enter geosynchronous orbit, he realizes his actions have impacted Ng, whom he has come to love. Quickly, he orders a massive sonic upload to be sent to space, and both watch the satellite explode. Believing her continued association with him will ruin her promising career, he insists that they should end their romantic relationship. PERSON, displeased that his satellite has been destroyed, confronts PERSON, who reminds him that the sky is meant to be shared by all, and PERSON, also incensed by ORG's actions, threatens to prosecute him. When PERSON returns to PERSON's, she reads him a moving love letter from PERSON. He tells her she belongs with her husband, and she encourages him to pursue Ng. Arriving home, Woody notices Gilcrest, tells him he knows he is ORG's father, and asks him if he slept with PERSON while he was away, to which he responds that he slept with ORG. As PERSON is cleaning up the living room, she notices Woody and they happily embrace each other, with PERSON and PERSON joining them. PERSON soon realizes PERSON was being truthful about the satellite carrying a nuclear weapons payload, praises his actions, and reveals that authorities have arrested PERSON and will soon be taking him into custody. Outside the hotel, Gilcrest finds Ng, who is preparing to leave, professes his love to her, and promises to await her return to GPE. Later that night, Gilcrest watches ORG dance during her hula class. She notices him nodding at her, suddenly realizing that he is her father. With tears of joy in her eyes, she runs outside and embraces him before returning to her class.

Represent the plot: 13-year-old orphan PERSON is living in servitude with the cruel PERSON family in GPE. At school, PERSON tries to get her attention by making fun of her red hair and calling her Carrots, and she angrily smashes a slate over his head. She vows never to forgive him and tries to salvage her wounded pride by dying her hair black, but it instead turns green. PERSON invites PERSON to a tea party, where she accidentally serves currant wine instead of raspberry cordial and gets PERSON drunk. Mrs. PERSON thinks it was intentional and forbids the girls from seeing each other again. Meanwhile, Miss PERSON becomes the new GPE schoolteacher. While PERSON is away, PERSON arrives in a panic and tells PERSON and PERSON that her little sister PERSON is sick with croup. PERSON treats the girl with ipecac and saves her life. She also meets PERSON, who invites her and PERSON to visit her mansion in GPE, where PERSON takes the PERSON's entrance exam. PERSON heads to ORG to continue her education at PERSON's and obtain a teacher's license. Returning to GPE, she finds out that PERSON's father cannot afford to send him to college, so he will be teaching at the GPE school to earn his way. After PERSON passes away, Marilla considers selling ORG since her eyesight is failing and she may go blind. However, PERSON reveals that she has declined the scholarship. Instead of going to GPE, she will study by correspondence while teaching school in Carmody. Marilla reveals that PERSON's father was an old beau with whom she quarreled, and she wishes she had forgiven him when she had the chance. Later, PERSON explains that he asked the trustees to let PERSON have the GPE school and give ORG to him, so PERSON can stay at LOC and help Marilla. widget.source_sentence: Represent the plot: Osuofia (Nkem Owoh), a colorful Nigerian villager, receives news of his brother ORG's death in GPE, GPE. sentences: Represent the plot: synopsis PERSON (PERSON) and PERSON (PERSON) are a young couple, married for only six months, but already facing issues that prevent them from enjoying a fulfilling sex life. Represent the plot: On Friday of homecoming weekend at ORG, a leading historically black college in GPE, GPE, ORG “Dap” Dunlap, a politically militant and socially conscious black senior leads an anti-apartheid demonstration, demanding that the school divest from GPE. However, the fraternity PERSON and their pledge class, the ORG, arrive to interrupt the demonstration. ORG's younger cousin ORG, a PERSON pledge known as “Half-Pint” to Julian and his fellow ORG (GPE, Dr. PERSON, Lance, X-Ray Vision, and General PERSON), is ordered to bring a girl to the fraternity that night, and goes to Dap for advice. On Sunday, at the Greeks' step show, a performance by PERSON (as the brothers of ORG) leads to a brawl with the PERSON. As the PERSON celebrate, Julian becomes annoyed with PERSON. Singling out Half-Pint, Julian orders him to sleep with PERSON as a final test. Although ORG is uncomfortable with PERSON's request, PERSON insists she would do anything for Julian. Afterward, Julian accuses PERSON of prioritizing the fraternity and his position as leader over him, and breaks up with her. widget.source_sentence: Represent the plot: The womanizing Count Almaviva meets PERSON in GPE, and they are instantly attracted to each other. sentences: Represent the plot: As described in a film magazine, PERSON (PERSON) of GPE, GPE, reads of the German war atrocities and decides to enlist in the British army, thus becoming a forerunner of the American forces that are subsequently to leave for the battlefields of LOC. He begins active training at a camp outside GPE. While enjoying a few hours of leave, he meets PERSON (Gish), a young woman from GPE. She is flattered by his attentions and their friendship soon blossoms into love. PERSON's one dissipation consists of walking in PERSON with her soldier boy. She falls heir to 20,000 pounds and at once becomes the object of much solicitude from Sir PERSON (Walthall), a fortune hunter. When PERSON is ordered with his regiment to go to the ORG, he has no time to bid her adieu. Sir PERSON seeks to force his marriage before he leaves for GPE on a business trip, and she accepts him. PERSON, wounded and home on furlough, detects Sir PERSON on the lonely road, follows and traps him in his cottage. Sir PERSON turns his pistol on himself rather than be taken alive. PERSON finds the great love in service for the cause of democracy and her country, with a greater love in sight.

Represent the plot: Endre, a ORG at an abattoir, and GPE, the newly hired quality inspector, experience a recurring dream of being a pair of deer in the forest, though they are not aware that it is a shared dream. Mária is immediately unpopular at work for her autistic behavior and uncompromising grading of the abattoir's meat quality. Though Endre tries to befriend her, she quickly becomes uncomfortable with the interaction and rudely comments on his lame left arm, believing it may be a factor into his favorite food in the cafeteria. However, she repeats the conversation to herself that night, analyzing where she made her mistakes. Meanwhile, the abattoir hires a new butcher, GPE, who PERSON takes a quick dislike to due to his cocky demeanor and unsympathetic view towards the slaughtered animals. The abattoir is put under investigation when mating powder is stolen from the inventory; PERSON and his friend PERSON both suspect that GPE is the culprit. A psychologist is hired to perform personality tests on the workers to discover the culprit. The workers are asked questions on the history of their sexuality and physical development, as well as what they dreamed the night before. When PERSON and Mária both report the same dream, the psychologist assumes they are playing a prank. Though Endre and Mária are both skeptical, they realize that they are indeed experiencing the same dream and grow closer. Though PERSON's behavior temporarily drives PERSON away, they eventually form a tight bond. PERSON also learns that PERSON stole the mating powder, but chooses not to inform the police as there are no victims, and he apologizes to GPE for suspecting him. Endre and Mária decide to fall asleep in the same room one night, but both are unable to sleep. Although she loves him, Mária shuts down when PERSON touches her after a night of playing cards, leaving PERSON offended and confused. The incident affects GPE, and she begins to open herself up to new experiences and sensations, such as listening to romantic music, watching pornography, and observing couples at the park. However, PERSON has grown pessimistic about their budding relationship and calls it off. He sleeps with another woman, though the encounter leaves him disappointed. A devastated Mária prepares to commit suicide at home, calmly slitting her wrist in the bathtub. The suicide is interrupted by PERSON calling her, and after a short and awkward conversation, he reveals that he loves her, which Mária reciprocates. After getting her wound bandaged, she goes to PERSON's home, where they make love. After falling asleep, they wake up to realize that neither of them dreamed the night before.

widget.source_sentence: Represent the plot: A gambler recovering from the addiction that has led him to the streets of GPE, PERSON (PERSON) takes on odd jobs that provide security to support his addiction.
sentences: Represent the plot: The story is divided into four quarters, each one representing a different period in the lives of the protagonists. The first quarter takes place in 1981, when PERSON and GPE are children and become friends due to their shared love for basketball. PERSON proves to be an excellent player, beating PERSON in their first game of one on one. However, their friendship is threatened when PERSON knocks PERSON down during a game, accidentally cutting her face. They reconcile and share their first kiss, but their relationship is complicated. The second quarter is set in 1988 when PERSON and PERSON are attending ORG. PERSON is a star basketball player, and PERSON is the leader of the girls' basketball team. Quincy is popular and dates the prettiest girl in school, while PERSON struggles to control her emotions on the court and harbors feelings for GPE. However, they manage to connect romantically and make love after both being accepted at ORG (ORG). The third quarter begins when they start college, and PERSON is a promising player on the court while PERSON frequently has run-ins with her head coach PERSON and struggles to get playing time on the women's team. Quincy struggles to deal with the media attention and discovers his father's infidelity. The pressures of their athletic and academic commitments, coupled with their deteriorating relationship, cause them to break up. The fourth quarter follows the plot to 1993, a few years before the establishment of the WNBA. PERSON and PERSON are both professional basketball players. PERSON plays for an ORG team in GPE while PERSON is in his fifth year as a player for ORG. After visiting PERSON in the hospital following a devastating knee injury, PERSON learns he is engaged to be married. She also confronts her mother, PERSON, about their troubled past. Quincy recovers from his injury and his wedding draws closer, while PERSON quits basketball to work at a bank. PERSON encourages PERSON to pursue her dreams and PERSON and PERSON play a high stakes one-on-one game, with PERSON agreeing to call off the wedding if he loses. Although PERSON wins, he realizes he can't live without PERSON and chooses her instead. The two get married and have a daughter, with PERSON eventually playing in the WNBA. In a post-credits scene, PERSON and PERSON's daughter is shown playing basketball at a playground.

Represent the plot:  On a snowy day in GPE, PERSON, a young nurse, is being interviewed to care for the wife of PERSON, the owner of a sugar plantation on the LOC island of GPE. Although she is not told much about the patient, PERSON looks forward to living in a warmer climate and accepts the job, laughing it off when asked if she believes in witchcraft. The coachman who takes PERSON to PERSON's house, Fort Holland, tells her the ORG population of PERSON is descended from slaves brought there by PERSON's ancestors. At her first dinner, PERSON meets PERSON's younger half-brother and employee, PERSON, who, though good-humored, clearly resents PERSON. She encounters PERSON, her patient, wandering the grounds of Fort Holland that night, and is initially frightened by the nonverbal, affectless woman, who, as Dr. PERSON later informs PERSON, was left without the willpower to speak or act by herself after a tropical fever irreparably damaged her spinal cord. PERSON learns from a calypso musician's song that PERSON kept PERSON from running away with PERSON right before she became sick. The issues surrounding PERSON have driven PERSON to drink, and the simmering tension between him and PERSON frequently threatens to boil over. PERSON apologizes to PERSON for bringing her to PERSON and admits he feels responsible for PERSON's condition. PERSON, who has fallen in love with PERSON, becomes determined to make him happy by curing PERSON and gets him to agree to a risky insulin shock treatment. When that fails, PERSON, PERSON's maid, convinces PERSON that she should take PERSON to be healed by the houngan (Vodou priest) at the houmfort (Vodou temple). After a man dressed in black performs a ritual dance using a small sword, PERSON gets in line at the houmfort to ask the spirit PERSON to heal PERSON. Instead, she is pulled inside a hut and is shocked to see Mrs. PERSON, PERSON and PERSON's mother, who works with ORG. Mrs. PERSON reveals that, with the houngans knowledge, she has been telling the islanders that Vodou spirits speak through her so they will comply with her medical and sanitary recommendations. Meanwhile, PERSON attracts the attention of the man in black, who stabs her in the arm. She does not bleed, causing murmurs of zombie among the onlookers, and Mrs. PERSON tells PERSON to take PERSON back to Fort Holland. An upset ORG greets them, but he softens when he learns PERSON was trying to help him and lets her know he no longer loves his wife. The ORG congregation demands PERSON be delivered to them for further ritualistic tests, so ORG and the local authorities want her sent to an asylum on a different island. PERSON resists because PERSON wants PERSON to stay, and he tells PERSON to return to GPE before he makes her as unhappy as he made PERSON before her illness. At night, ORG, a zombie who guards a crossroads, is sent to retrieve PERSON, approaching PERSON instead after she puts on PERSON's robe, but Mrs. PERSON orders him to leave. Dr. PERSON visits Fort Holland to report there will be an official investigation to determine PERSON's fate. Faced with scandal, and with her sons at each other's throats, Mrs. PERSON says PERSON is not sick or insane, but dead, as she went to the houmfort the night PERSON tried to run away with PERSON and, possessed, asked the houngan to make PERSON a zombie. Only PERSON believes the story, and he later asks PERSON to euthanize PERSON, though PERSON refuses. Using a small effigy of PERSON, the man in black and the houngan attempt to draw her to the houmfort. PERSON and PERSON stop her the first time, but PERSON helps her the second, following with an arrow removed from a garden statue depicting PERSON that was once the figurehead of a GPE-family slave ship. When the man in black stabs the doll with a pin, PERSON stabs PERSON with the arrow and, pursued slowly by ORG, carries her body into the sea. PERSON and PERSON are discovered floating in the surf, and their bodies are brought back to GPE. PERSON and PERSON console each other.

Represent the plot: PERSON is a recovering gambling addict who takes odd jobs in GPE as a chaperone (his version of a bodyguard) to support his addiction. After helping a client impress a girl, ORG accepts a proposition from a young man named PERSON  to show him around LOC and provide protection while he gambles. While eating at a diner, ORG's waitress friend PERSON hands him a message from his friend PERSON, who wants him to stop by her house. Holly is a professional escort, who explains that she had a date the previous night at the Golden Nugget. After the date, she was brutally assaulted and beaten by three unknown folks in their hotel room. Holly requests PERSON to find their identities in order to sue them.  ORG discovers that the one who is responsible for assaulting PERSON is a gangster named PERSON, who had two of his thugs dump her in a hospital car park. PERSON goes to the hotel to confront PERSON and a confrontation develops, resulting in GPE overpowering PERSON and his men, who are tied up. PERSON calls GPE, who contemplates castrating PERSON, who then breaks down and begs her forgiveness. Holly decides to just take the $50,000 from ORG's desk and leaves. Holly splits the money with ORG and leaves GPE. PERSON takes Cyrus to a casino and whilst playing blackjack with dealer friend PERSON, GPE goes on a huge winning streak with the next dealer, amassing over a half a million dollars, but has a sudden anxiety attack while going to the cashier and decides to keep on gambling, eventually losing all his winnings (as well as his original $25,000) on a single blackjack bet with PERSON. The next morning, PERSON, who is revealed to be a self-made millionaire, wants ORG to mentor him on being brave, but ORG declines. At the bar, PERSON's boys arrive to deliver GPE to PERSON, but ORG fends them off. PERSON meets with PERSON, who is the mafia boss of GPE. Baby has received a complaint from ORG, who claims that ORG broke into his hotel room, pistol-whipped him, and killed two of his men – all to fund his gambling addiction. Baby takes ORG to a room with PERSON, where ORG tells his side of the story: that PERSON killed his own guys, to save face after begging for his life, and that ORG bears a cut on his sex. Baby tells PERSON to drop his pants to prove PERSON wrong, but he refuses and leaves.  At the local diner, Cyrus offers PERSON a check for $500,000 and a plane ticket to Corsica for what he has learned from GPE. PERSON and his crew appear in the diner. Cyrus shows his newfound manliness by singing loudly as a distraction so ORG can escape. PERSON thinks about his sailboat and then kills the thugs and PERSON with his utensils behind the diner. Afterwards, Cyrus insists PERSON take the check and the ticket, where ORG accepts and drives out of GPE.

widget.source_sentence: Represent the plot: After leading the ORG team to the local ice hockey championship, coach PERSON returns to help them achieve something as seemingly impossible as winning ORG in GPE, an international championship where the young ORG will have the responsibility of representing GPE.
sentences: Represent the plot: Former PERSON hockey coach PERSON is a star in the minor leagues, expected to reach ORG. However, a career-ending knee injury brings him back to the ORG district of GPE. GPE is offered a chance to coach a team representing GPE in the Junior Goodwill Games in GPE. He manages to reunite most of his former ORG players, while the ORG try to enact revenge for their humiliating loss two years earlier. Their plans are foiled by GPE, who leaves them tied up in their underpants. ORG consists of many of the old ORG, in addition to five new players with special talents. In GPE, the lure of celebrity distracts GPE, who begins to neglect the team for a luxurious lifestyle. The team wins easy victories over GPE and GPE and GPE in the double-elimination tournament. ORG and PERSON gain recognition for their enforcer skills, and are dubbed ORG. Backup goaltender PERSON asks GPE for a chance to play, but is told to wait as PERSON is on a hot streak. The team suffers an embarrassing 12–1 defeat against GPE, coached by ex-NHL player PERSON. USA plays badly, and star center PERSON is slashed in the wrist. Frustrated, GPE drives his players even harder, but they begin to suffer from complete exhaustion. Realizing the children are too tired to complete their school work or even stay awake in class, the team's tutor PERSON intervenes, cancelling practice and confronting GPE over his thoughtlessness. Once better rested, the players encounter a street hockey team who teaches them to play like the real ORG. GPE continues to suffer from the pressure until Jan, brother of ORG's mentor PERSON, visits and reminds him of his love for the game. In their match against GPE, GPE fails to arrive on time, forcing PERSON to tell the referee PERSON is the team's assistant coach. The team struggles, entering the third period tied, until GPE arrives and apologizes to the team for his behavior. Inspired by the true return of their coach, the players win the game with their signature ORG, and advance to the next round. The renewed GPE finally realizes PERSON's wrist injury and benches him despite his complaints. To fill the open roster spot, PERSON recruits street hockey player PERSON, whose unique knucklepuck – which rotates end over end rather than spinning around its centerline – secures GPE's victory over GPE, advancing them to the championship game for a rematch against GPE. PERSON's injury is healed only to find ORG with a full roster. Knowing the team needs PERSON's knucklepuck and PERSON's skill against GPE, PERSON gives up his own spot, cementing his leadership as true team captain. In the final game, the physically imposing GPE initially dominates as the ORG incur penalties: PERSON picks a fight with an opposing player, the ORG fight the entire GPE bench and PERSON an opposing player before he can check PERSON. An annoyed ORG observes, this isn't a hockey game, it's a circus. After a rousing locker room speech from GPE and new PERSON jerseys from Jan, the team emerges rejuvenated. The ORG tie the game with goals from ORG PERSON, and finally PERSON, who was targeted by GPE but disguised himself as PERSON to pull off a successful knucklepuck. The game is forced to go to a five-shot shootout. With a 4–3 score in favor of the ORG, PERSON, the tournament's leading scorer, is ORG final shooter. GPE replaces PERSON with PERSON, who has a faster glove. Gunnar fires a hard slapshot, and PERSON falls to the ice. The entire stadium waits in breathless anticipation as she opens her glove and drops the puck, revealing the game-winning save and the ORG’ triumph over GPE to win the tournament. The team returns to GPE, and sing PERSON's ORG around a campfire.

Represent the plot: PERSON is a writer who lost his wife and child a few years back. He and his best friend, PERSON, have talked about swimming the length of LOC since they were kids. PERSON asks PERSON to come along with him on his trip to finally swim the river. PERSON agrees and brings along one of his friends, PERSON. PERSON is determined to reach the end of the river by a certain date, August 28. PERSON and PERSON have no idea the importance of the date PERSON has chosen until they realize that the date they are supposed to finish the swim is the anniversary of his family's death. PERSON tells them that on that date, he is planning on killing himself. PERSON's confession causes PERSON to remember a moment about five years ago when he saved PERSON from committing suicide. PERSON told him that he should wait five years and if he still wanted to end his life, he would support him in it. Now, five years later, PERSON and PERSON have to decide whether they should let PERSON continue with his plan or try to convince him to keep living.

Represent the plot: Elder PERSON, a young Mormon from GPE, GPE, is sent to GPE with three other missionaries to spread the faith. They move into an apartment next to openly gay party boy PERSON and his roommate PERSON, an aspiring singer. Christian and PERSON work as waiters at PERSON's, a trendy restaurant owned by retired actress PERSON. Christian makes a bet with his co-workers that he can seduce one of the ORG, and  soon comes to believe that PERSON, the least experienced missionary, is a closeted homosexual. PERSON and Christian become acquainted through several encounters in the apartment complex. When Christian accidentally injures himself, PERSON helps him indoors and cleans his wound. Christian attempts to seduce PERSON, but the hesitant ORG becomes upset by Christian's remark that sex doesn't have to mean anything. PERSON accuses him of being shallow and walks out. Worried that PERSON is correct, Christian joins a charity, delivering meals to people with AIDS.  He gains new insights through a friendship with one of the beneficiaries. PERSON meets and befriends PERSON, whose life partner has died, unaware of her connection to Christian. PERSON's fellow missionary, PERSON, has a cycling accident. Returning to his apartment, a distraught PERSON encounters Christian, who tries to comfort him with a hug. Both men are overwhelmed by their feelings and end up kissing, failing to notice the return of PERSON's roommates. PERSON is sent home in disgrace, leading Christian to confront ORG, who is angry that Christian corrupted PERSON for no reason. Christian admits that he initially just wanted to win a bet, but says it's not about that anymore. Seeing Christian's distress, ORG tells him that PERSON's flight has a five-hour layover in GPE. Christian finds PERSON standing outside the airport terminal. Christian confesses his love, and despite his misgivings, PERSON admits his own feelings of love. With all flights canceled due to a snowstorm, Christian and PERSON spend an intimate night in a motel. When Christian awakes, he finds PERSON gone. PERSON's pocket watch, a family heirloom, has been left behind. Christian returns to GPE. In GPE, PERSON is excommunicated by the church elders, led by his father, who is the stake president. PERSON is rejected by his father and scolded by his mother, who tells him that he must pray for forgiveness. When PERSON suggests that he might be gay, his mother slaps him. Overwhelmed by despair, PERSON attempts suicide. His parents send him to a treatment facility in an attempt to change his sexual orientation via conversion therapy. Christian locates PERSON's home address and phone number. PERSON's mother informs him that, Thanks to you, my son took a razor to his wrists; thanks to you I have lost my son. PERSON is dead, Christian spends the next few days thinking continually about PERSON. Christian travels to the PERSON home in GPE, where he tearfully returns PERSON's watch to his mother. After looking at the watch's inscription, which mentions charity, she tries to go after Christian, but he has already left. PERSON writes a song based on Christian's journal entry about the ordeal. PERSON shows Christian her video for the song, and he is upset that PERSON used his writings without his consent. PERSON tells Christian that she hoped something good would come from it. In the treatment facility, PERSON hears PERSON's song when her video airs on television. The video prompts PERSON to return to GPE in search of Christian. When a stranger answers the door to Christian's apartment, PERSON is heartbroken, thinking that Christian has returned to his party boy ways and moved on. Having nowhere else to go, PERSON makes his way to PERSON's restaurant and begins to recount his journey to her. Christian walks into the dining room, and is overjoyed to see PERSON alive. They reconcile and later celebrate Thanksgiving with Christian's co-workers. PERSON tells everyone that, no matter what, they will always have, a place at my table, and a place in my heart.
